identity.short_name: Chinese remainder synthesis (construct a unique residue class) - Buffed Seed

problem_spec.summary: Construct a CRT-synthesized unit modulo a mixed modulus containing a nontrivial prime power, then determine the least positive exponent that forces a prescribed local power-pattern; this requires translating power congruences into exponent congruences via multiplicative orders and then recombining them by CRT, with the final answer reported modulo 1000.
givens: $g$ is a primitive root modulo $q$, i.e. $\operatorname{ord}_q(g)=q-1$.
Let $A$ be the unique residue class modulo $M$ such that $A\equiv 1+p\pmod{p^k}$ and $A\equiv g\pmod q$, and take $A\in\{0,1,\dots,M-1\}$ to be its least nonnegative representative.
ask:
Let \(A\) be the least nonnegative integer with \(A\equiv 1+p\pmod{p^k}\) and \(A\equiv g\pmod q\), where \(g\) is a primitive root modulo \(q\), and set \(M=p^kq\).
Define \(S=\{n\in\mathbb Z_{>0} : A^n\equiv 1\pmod{p^k}\ 	ext{and}\ A^n\equiv -1\pmod q\}\).
Prove \(S
eqarnothing\), let \(n_0=\min S\), and report \(n_0  mod 1000\) (an integer in \(\{0,1,\dots,999\}\)).

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: supplies the prime-power factor $p^k$ and the nontrivial order constraint via $1+p$ modulo $p^k$

parameter(s).name: k
parameter(s).purpose: forces a nontrivial prime power modulus and ensures the exponent modulus $p^{k-1}$ is itself a genuine prime power with $\ge 9$

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: ensures a cyclic unit group of size $q-1$ that is coprime to $p^{k-1}$, enabling a clean CRT step on exponent congruences

parameter(s).name: g
parameter(s).purpose: fixes a generator modulo $q$ so that the condition $A^n\equiv -1\pmod q$ becomes a rigid congruence condition on $n$

invariant(s).name: structural_CRT_modulus
invariant(s).statement: The modulus has the structured form $M=p^kq$ with $k\ge 3$ and $p,q$ distinct odd primes, so $\gcd(p^k,q)=1$ and at least one local modulus is a nontrivial prime power (not all moduli are prime).

invariant(s).name: anti_trivial_nontrivial_orders
invariant(s).statement: Since $k\ge 3$ and $q>3$, both $p^{k-1}>1$ and $q-1>2$, so neither local power condition collapses to a tautology and the extremal value $n_0$ cannot be $1$.

invariant(s).name: order_of_one_plus_p_mod_p_k
invariant(s).statement: In $(\mathbb{Z}/p^k\mathbb{Z})^\times$, the element $1+p$ has exact multiplicative order $p^{k-1}$.

invariant(s).name: primitive_root_half_turn
invariant(s).statement: Since $\operatorname{ord}_q(g)=q-1$ and $q$ is odd, $g^{(q-1)/2}\equiv -1\pmod q$, and $g^n\equiv -1\pmod q\iff n\equiv (q-1)/2\pmod{q-1}$.

invariant(s).name: coprime_exponent_moduli_for_CRT
invariant(s).statement: $\gcd(p^{k-1},q-1)=1$, hence the simultaneous congruences $n\equiv 0\pmod{p^{k-1}}$ and $n\equiv (q-1)/2\pmod{q-1}$ admit a unique solution class modulo $p^{k-1}(q-1)$.

invariant(s).name: unit_invertibility
invariant(s).statement: $\gcd(A,M)=1$ (since $A\equiv 1+p\not\equiv 0\pmod p$ and $A\equiv g\not\equiv 0\pmod q$), so multiplicative orders and exponent reductions are well-defined.

essential_assumption(s): The modulus interface is structurally constrained: $M=p^kq$ with $k\ge 3$ and $\gcd(p^k,q)=1$, so the CRT synthesis of $A$ is deterministic and includes a genuine prime-power factor.

The task cannot be solved by a single CRT reconstruction step on $A$; one must first use multiplicative-order reasoning to translate the power constraints on $A^n$ into congruences on the exponent $n$, and only then apply CRT to those exponent congruences to obtain the extremal value $n_0$.
Anti-triviality guards $k\ge 3$ and $q>3$ ensure both exponent moduli $p^{k-1}$ and $q-1$ exceed $2$, ruling out degenerate cases such as order $=1$ or $-1\equiv 1$.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Using $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(A)=p^{k-1}$ (since $A\equiv 1+p\pmod{p^k}$), we have $A^n\equiv 1\pmod{p^k}\iff n\equiv 0\pmod{p^{k-1}}$. Using $\operatorname{ord}_q(A)=q-1$ (since $A\equiv g\pmod q$ with $g$ primitive), we have $A^n\equiv -1\pmod q\iff n\equiv (q-1)/2\pmod{q-1}$. Because $\gcd(p^{k-1},q-1)=1$, CRT yields a unique residue class $n\pmod{p^{k-1}(q-1)}$ satisfying both; therefore $S$ is exactly one arithmetic progression and has a unique smallest positive element $n_0$. The reported output is the canonical residue $n_0\bmod 1000\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$, which is unique because $n_0$ itself is unique.

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder
technique(s).purpose: synthesize the global unit $A\pmod{p^kq}$ from its prescribed local residues
outputs: a unique residue class $A\pmod M$ and its least nonnegative representative

technique(s).name: order_translation
technique(s).purpose: translate power congruences for $A^n$ into congruences on the exponent $n$ (bridge from ORDER to CRT)

technique(s).name: CRT_on_order_constraints
technique(s).purpose: combine ORDER-derived exponent constraints into a single congruence class and extract the least positive representative (interaction kernel: ORDER + CRT)
outputs: unique class $n\pmod{p^{k-1}(q-1)}$ and the extremal value $n_0=\min S$

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
theorem(s).role: guarantees existence/uniqueness of $A\pmod{p^kq}$ from its residues modulo $p^k$ and $q$, and guarantees existence/uniqueness of the exponent class modulo $p^{k-1}(q-1)$

theorem(s).name: order_of_1_plus_p_mod_prime_power
theorem(s).role: gives $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$ (typically proved by binomial expansion / lifting arguments)

theorem(s).name: primitive_root_mod_prime
theorem(s).role: ensures $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic and that $\operatorname{ord}_q(g)=q-1$ makes $-1$ the half-power $g^{(q-1)/2}$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: A congruence constraint is a statement about membership in an arithmetic progression; CRT intersects progressions into a single progression.
In a finite cyclic group, a power equation $u^n=v$ becomes a congruence condition on $n$ once the order structure is identified.

critical_trick(s): Use the known exact order $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$ to rewrite $A^n\equiv 1\pmod{p^k}$ as the exponent constraint $n\equiv 0\pmod{p^{k-1}}$.
Use that $A\equiv g\pmod q$ with $g$ primitive to rewrite $A^n\equiv -1\pmod q$ as $n\equiv (q-1)/2\pmod{q-1}$.
Apply CRT to these ORDER-derived exponent congruences (made possible by $\gcd(p^{k-1},q-1)=1$), then take the least positive representative and finally normalize modulo $1000$.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic (gcd, pow) for fast modular checks.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Optional verification: crt reconstruction, primitive-root checks, and multiplicative-order computation on small admissible samples.

import_hint(s): from sympy.ntheory.residue_ntheory import is_primitive_root, primitive_root, n_order

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: For small admissible parameters, compute $A$ via crt, solve the exponent congruences via CRT, take the least positive $n_0$, and verify $A^{n_0}\equiv 1\pmod{p^k}$ and $A^{n_0}\equiv -1\pmod q$.

task(s).name: order_sanity_check
task(s).reason: For small odd primes $p$ and $k\ge 3$, verify $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$ by direct multiplicative-order computation (n_order).

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independently brute-force the minimal $n_0$ for small parameter sizes to confirm agreement with the CRT-derived value.
